articles.title: Mercury crosses 48°C in R’than’s Barmer: Hail hits MP & Haryana; trees, poles crush vehicles in Bareilly; s... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Mercury crosses 48°C in R’than’s Barmer:Hail hits MP & Haryana; trees, poles crush vehicles in Bareilly; storms, lightning kill 11 in Bihar, M’rashtra
- Copy link
Rajasthan recorded extreme heat on Tuesday, with temperatures crossing 45°C in five districts. Barmer remained the hottest city in the country for the third consecutive day, recording 48.3°C. Other high temperatures included Phalodi at 46.4°C, Jaisalmer at 45.8°C, Chittorgarh at 45.6°C, and Bikaner at 45°C.
Madhya Pradesh experienced contrasting weather conditions. While Ratlam remained the hottest city in the state for the third consecutive day, with temperatures reaching 46.5°C, hailstorms accompanied by strong winds and rain were reported in Bijour and Niwari districts.
Hailstorms were also reported in Pingawa, Haryana. Heavy rainfall affected Panchkula, Yamunanagar, Karnal and Gurugram. Meanwhile, dust storms with speeds of up to 50 kmph swept through Bareilly and Rampur in Uttar Pradesh, uprooting roadside poles and causing tree branches to fall onto moving vehicles.
In Bihar’s Jamui and Banka districts, five people, including a child, died due to lightning strikes. In Sangli, Maharashtra, six people lost their lives after a temple wall collapsed during a severe storm. In Kerala’s Malappuram, four people were killed in lightning-related incidents.
Weather forecast for the next two days
May 14:
May 15:
Monsoon may reach Andaman-Nicobar this week
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the Southwest Monsoon may reach the South Bay of Bengal, Andaman Sea, and Andaman & Nicobar Islands by the end of this week.
The low-pressure area formed over the Southwest Bay of Bengal is still active in the same region. The associated cyclonic circulation extends up to about 4.5 kilometers above sea level. The department said that this system could strengthen further in the next 48 hours.
Typically, the monsoon reaches Andaman and Nicobar around May 20. The monsoon hits Kerala on June 1. With this, the Southwest Monsoon season is considered to begin in the country, which lasts until September.
5 Pictures related to weather from across the country
Weather news from states
1. Rajasthan: First red alert for heat in the season, Barmer continues to be the hottest city in the country for the 3rd consecutive day
Barmer remained the hottest city in the country for the third consecutive day. The day temperature here was 48.3°C. This is the highest temperature of the season so far. The Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for heatwave in 3 districts on Wednesday. An advisory has also been issued for people not to leave their homes during the day.
2. Madhya Pradesh: Heatwave for next 4 days in Indore-Ujjain division, alert in 11 districts; Bhopal-Narmadapuram will also be affected
11 out of 15 districts in the Indore and Ujjain divisions of the state will be hit by heatwave for the next 4 days. Heatwave will also prevail in Bhopal and Narmadapuram. On Tuesday, hailstones also fell with rain in Bijour of Niwari district. Meanwhile, a temperature of 46.5°C was recorded in Ratlam.
3. Bihar: Rain alert issued in 19 districts; five dead in storm, rain and lightning, while Kaimur records a high of 41°C.
The Meteorological Department has predicted rain across the state today. A rain alert has been issued for 19 districts. In the last 24 hours, heavy rain occurred in 5 districts including Katihar, Gayaji, and Jehanabad. In Jamui, 4 people, including a child, died due to storm, rain, and lightning. Meanwhile, the temperature in Kaimur was recorded at 41°C.
4. Himachal Pradesh: Alert for storm, rain, and hailstones in three districts, 35% more rain than normal in May
The state has received 35% more rainfall than normal so far in May, which is 9% above average. According to the Meteorological Department, there is no relief from rain expected for the next three days. Today, there is an alert for thunderstorms, rain, and hailstones in Kullu, Mandi, and Shimla. During this period, winds can blow at a speed of 50-60kmph.
5. Punjab: Thunderstorm and rain alert in 3 districts, temperature crosses 40°C in two districts, Faridkot hottest
The heat has started increasing once again in the state. The temperature crossed 40C in two districts. A maximum temperature of 43C was recorded in Faridkot and 40.1C in Bathinda. According to the Meteorological Department, there is a storm and rain alert in Pathankot, Hoshiarpur, and Rupnagar, which border Himachal. During this, winds will blow at a speed of 40-50kmph.
6. Uttarakhand: Rain alert in 5 districts including Uttarkashi-Chamoli, winds will blow at 60kmph; possibility of hailstones in plain areas
There is a rain alert in all districts of the state today. The Meteorological Department has issued an orange alert for Uttarkashi, Rudraprayag, Chamoli, Bageshwar, and Pithoragarh. During this period, winds can blow at a speed of 40-60kmph. Snowfall may occur in areas above 4200 meters. Meanwhile, hailstones are expected in the plains.
